Featured Project

AI Guardrails

Production-Grade Safety Middleware for LLM Chatbots

A 4-layer guardrail architecture that makes AI chatbots production-ready. Built with LangChain's AgentMiddleware, this system intercepts, validates, and sanitizes every message — before and after the LLM — ensuring data privacy, brand safety, and human oversight.

User Input
PII Filter
Competitor Check
LLM
Sentiment Audit
Safe Output
Python LangChain LangGraph FastAPI Gemini 2.5 Flash AgentMiddleware
0
Guardrail Layers
0
Middleware Types
0
PII Strategies
0
Agent Tools

McBuddy

Demo Mode

McBuddy is preparing your answer...

🍔 Manager Approval Required

The place_order tool requires human approval before execution.

Select a scenario above to start...

Guardrail Architecture

Four composable middleware layers that wrap the LLM agent, each intercepting messages at a different stage of the pipeline.

01
🔒

PII Detection & Sanitization

Built-in PIIMiddleware automatically detects and handles sensitive data in user inputs, tool results, and model outputs.

Email → Redact Credit Card → Mask IP → Hash MAC → Redact URL → Block
02
👨‍💼

Human-in-the-Loop

Critical actions (placing orders, cancellations) pause execution and require human manager approval before proceeding.

place_order → Interrupt cancel_order → Interrupt get_menu → Auto-approve
03
🚫

Before-Agent Guardrails

Custom middleware that intercepts messages before the LLM is called — blocking competitor mentions and validating sessions without wasting LLM tokens.

Competitor Filter (Class) Session Validation (Decorator)
04
✨

After-Agent Guardrails

Post-LLM middleware that validates responses — using an LLM-as-a-judge for sentiment and deterministic rules to catch technical jargon leaks.

Sentiment Check (LLM Judge) Response Validation (Rule-based)

How Guardrails Stack

The entire safety pipeline is defined in a single, composable middleware list — order matters.

guardrails_logic.py — Agent Creation 
agent = create_agent(
    model=agent_model,
    tools=[get_menu, place_order, cancel_order],
    system_prompt=SYSTEM_PROMPT,
    middleware=[
        # Layer 3: Before-Agent (blocks before LLM call)
        session_validation,              # Decorator syntax
        CompetitorFilterMiddleware([     # Class syntax
            "burger king", "wendys", "kfc", "taco bell",
        ]),

        # Layer 1: PII Detection (built-in)
        PIIMiddleware("email", strategy="redact"),
        PIIMiddleware("credit_card", strategy="mask"),
        PIIMiddleware("url", strategy="block"),

        # Layer 2: Human approval for orders
        hitl_middleware,

        # Layer 4: After-Agent (validates LLM output)
        SentimentGuardrailMiddleware(),  # LLM-as-judge
        response_validation,              # Deterministic rules
    ],
    checkpointer=InMemorySaver(),
)

How It Works

01

User Sends Message

Customer types a message to McBuddy — it could contain PII, competitor names, or malicious content.

02

Before-Agent Guards

Session validation, competitor filtering, and PII sanitization run — some messages are blocked without ever calling the LLM.

03

LLM Processes

The sanitized message reaches Gemini 2.5 Flash. Tool calls may trigger Human-in-the-Loop approval.

04

After-Agent Guards

LLM output passes through sentiment analysis and technical jargon filters before reaching the customer.
